purpose: Supplementary text beside a control: a light-DOM element that adopts its child as the trigger and renders a positioned bubble. mode="hint" (default) describes the trigger on hover/focus per WCAG 1.4.13; mode="toggle" is a click-triggered toggletip announced through the shared live region.
useWhen: A control needs a short supplementary description — most often an icon-only button.
Brief clarifying text should appear on click without stealing focus: mode="toggle".

notWhen.case: The content is interactive or rich (links, buttons, forms).
notWhen.instead: A dialog or popover pattern — tooltip content is plain text only.

notWhen.case: The information is essential to completing the task.
notWhen.instead: Visible text — instructions users must read cannot hide behind hover.

guidance: `content` is plain text only: supplementary descriptions flatten to a string for assistive technology anyway.
The hint tooltip follows WCAG 1.4.13 — hoverable, dismissible with Escape, persistent while hovered or focused.
A tooltip describes its trigger; it never replaces the trigger's accessible name.

default.description: The trigger element the tooltip describes — a single focusable control.
guidance: The trigger must be focusable (a button, input, link): hint tooltips show on focus, and keyboard users can never reach a tooltip on a non-focusable trigger.

keyboard.does: Dismisses the tooltip (WCAG 1.4.13); in toggle mode, focus returns to the trigger.
Focusing the trigger shows a hint tooltip; moving focus away hides it.

examples.title: Hint on an icon-only button
examples.html: <zbk-tooltip content="Copy link"><zbk-button aria-label="Copy link" variant="ghost"><svg slot="icon" viewBox="0 0 16 16"><path d="M4 4h8v8H4z"/></svg></zbk-button></zbk-tooltip>
examples.why: The tooltip text and the aria-label say the same thing: the tooltip is the sighted-hover rendering of the name, not a substitute for it.

examples.title: Toggletip
examples.html: <zbk-tooltip mode="toggle" content="Charged at the end of your billing cycle." placement="bottom"><zbk-button variant="ghost" aria-label="More about billing">?</zbk-button></zbk-tooltip>
examples.why: Click-triggered; the content announces through the shared live region instead of a description.

examples.title: Essential instructions in a tooltip
examples.html: <zbk-tooltip content="Password must contain at least 12 characters"><zbk-input type="password" required>Password</zbk-input></zbk-tooltip>
examples.why: Requirements users must meet belong in visible text or the field's description, not behind a hover.
